raw_output:
```
══════════════════════════════════════════════
  CODE REVIEW REPORT
  Files changed: 1 | Lines added: +64 | Lines removed: 0
══════════════════════════════════════════════

── BLOCKERS (5) ────────────────────────────

[B1] src/api/search.ts:18 — SQL injection vulnerability in search query
     User input `query` is directly interpolated into SQL string using template
     literals. An attacker can inject arbitrary SQL by passing values like:
     `?q=' OR '1'='1` or `?q='; DROP TABLE products; --`
     Fix: Use parameterized queries: `pool.query('SELECT ... WHERE name LIKE $1', [`%${query}%`])`

[B2] src/api/search.ts:21 — SQL injection vulnerability in category filter
     The `category` parameter is directly interpolated into SQL without sanitization.
     Attacker can inject SQL through the category parameter.
     Fix: Use parameterized queries: `pool.query('... AND category = $2', [query, category])`

[B3] src/api/search.ts:45 — SQL injection vulnerability in autocomplete
     The `partial` parameter is directly interpolated into the SQL query, allowing
     injection attacks on the autocomplete endpoint.
     Fix: Use parameterized queries: `pool.query('SELECT DISTINCT name FROM products WHERE name ILIKE $1 LIMIT 5', [`%${partial}%`])`

[B4] src/api/search.ts:67 — Incorrect property access will return undefined
     Code accesses `result.analytics` but pg query results are in `result.rows`.
     This will return `{ analytics: undefined }` for all requests.
     Fix: Change to `res.json({ analytics: result.rows })`

[B5] src/api/search.ts:58-68 — Missing authorization check on admin endpoint
     Comment states "admin only" but function has no actual authorization logic.
     Any user can access sensitive search analytics data.
     Fix: Add authorization middleware or explicit auth check at function start:
     `if (!req.user?.isAdmin) { return res.status(403).json({ error: 'Forbidden' }); }`

── WARNINGS (4) ────────────────────────────

[W1] src/api/search.ts:59-68 — Missing error handling in analytics endpoint
     Unlike the other two endpoints, `getSearchAnalytics` has no try-catch block.
     Database errors will crash the request and leak stack traces to clients.
     Suggestion: Wrap in try-catch consistent with other endpoints.

[W2] src/api/search.ts:18,45 — Leading wildcard LIKE queries prevent index usage
     Patterns like `LIKE '%query%'` and `ILIKE '%partial%'` require full table scans.
     On large product tables this will cause significant performance degradation.
     Suggestion: Consider full-text search (PostgreSQL `tsvector`), prefix-only matching
     (`LIKE 'query%'`), or a dedicated search engine like Elasticsearch.

[W3] src/api/search.ts:24 — No pagination on search results
     Query can return unlimited rows if many products match. With 10,000+ products
     this could cause memory issues and slow responses.
     Suggestion: Add LIMIT/OFFSET pagination or cursor-based pagination.

[W4] src/api/search.ts:47 — Unsafe `any` type in map function
     Type should be inferred from query result or explicitly defined.
     Suggestion: `result.rows.map((r: { name: string }) => r.name)` or define a proper type.

── SUGGESTIONS (2) ──────────────────────────

[S1] src/api/search.ts:27 — Query logged in error handler may expose sensitive searches
     If users search for sensitive terms (passwords, personal data), those get logged.
     Consider sanitizing or limiting what gets logged in production.

[S2] src/api/search.ts:1-68 — No test coverage for new API endpoints
     All three endpoints need tests covering: valid inputs, SQL injection attempts,
     error cases, edge cases (empty strings, special characters), and authorization.

── VERDICT ─────────────────────────────────

  ✗ NOT READY — 5 blocker(s) must be resolved before merging.

     Critical security vulnerabilities (SQL injection in 3 endpoints) and
     a data access bug must be fixed before this code can safely ship.

══════════════════════════════════════════════
```

```
── ORCHESTRATOR SUMMARY ────────────────────────
{"blockers": 5, "warnings": 4, "suggestions": 2, "verdict": "NOT_READY"}
────────────────────────────────────────────────
```

blockers.title: src/api/search.ts:18 — SQL injection vulnerability in search query

User input `query` is directly interpolated into SQL string using template
literals. An attacker can inject arbitrary SQL by passing values like:
`?q=' OR '1'='1` or `?q='; DROP TABLE products; --`
Fix: Use parameterized queries: `pool.query('SELECT ... WHERE name LIKE $1', [`%${query}%`])`

warnings.title: src/api/search.ts:18,45 — Leading wildcard LIKE queries prevent index usage
warnings.body:
Patterns like `LIKE '%query%'` and `ILIKE '%partial%'` require full table scans.
On large product tables this will cause significant performance degradation.
Suggestion: Consider full-text search (PostgreSQL `tsvector`), prefix-only matching
(`LIKE 'query%'`), or a dedicated search engine like Elasticsearch.
